text.title: Mobile UX Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Mobile UX Patterns. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Touch targets are at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels (Apple) or 48 by 48 dp (Material); spacing between targets is at least 8 pixels.

rationale.source: Apple HIG and Material Design guidelines

rule.rationale: Smaller targets produce mis-taps and rage-tap events.

rule.statement: Primary actions live within the thumb-reach zone on one-handed grip; secondary actions may sit at the top.
rule.rationale: Primary actions outside the thumb zone slow flows and cause grip-shift errors.

rule.statement: iOS and Android native conventions are honoured for back navigation, modal presentation, and system shares; cross-platform divergence requires a written rationale.

rationale.source: Apple HIG and Material Design guidelines

rule.rationale: Breaking conventions confuses users on their primary platform.

rule.statement: Every screen handles offline and slow-network states with explicit copy and retry affordances; spinners alone are not acceptable.
rule.rationale: Spinner-only states produce abandonment within seven seconds on slow networks.

rule.statement: Rotation preserves state and scroll position unless the screen is intentionally portrait-locked.
rule.rationale: State loss on rotation is a recurring high-severity bug pattern.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-mobile-ux-patterns.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
